county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Platte County formed 1856 from Loup County (extinct). County seat is Columbus. Central Nebraska at the Loup-Platte confluence. Union Pacific Railroad. Strong Polish, Irish, German, and Czech settlement.
county_metadata.research_tips: Platte County (formed 1856, seat Columbus) records are at the courthouse in Columbus. Columbus is a major central Nebraska city at the confluence of the Loup and Platte rivers. The Platte County Historical Society has local materials. Columbus was a Union Pacific Railroad town and attracted diverse immigrant settlers. Strong Polish community in the Columbus area -- Polish Catholic parish records are essential. Irish, German, and Czech settlers were also significant. Homestead entries at BLM GLO and railroad land sales document early settlers. FamilySearch has extensive microfilm of county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Columbus newspapers from the 1870s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Platte County. County Register of Deeds in Columbus holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Platte County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Platte County Register of Deeds, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Platte County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.repository: Platte County Court, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1856. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Platte County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.repository: Platte County Court, Columbus
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1856. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Platte County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Platte County (1860-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1890 federal census destroyed nationally; Nebraska 1885 state census survives

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Platte County including homestead entries, pre-emption claims, timber culture entries, cash sales, and military warrant locations. Nebraska is a public land state -- all original land transfers from federal ownership are documented here. Searchable online by name and location.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions and burial records for Platte County. Find A Grave has growing coverage. Township, church, and family cemeteries are common in rural Nebraska. County historical societies and the Nebraska State Genealogical Society have published cemetery transcription volumes.

Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Platte County history.

source_profiles.source_name: Platte County Polish Catholic Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Omaha / Diocese of Grand Island / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Polish Catholic parish records for Platte County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Records often maintained in Latin with Polish surnames. FamilySearch has microfilmed some collections.

source_profiles.source_name: Platte County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: History Nebraska / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Platte County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, homestead proof notices, estate settlements, and community news. History Nebraska holds extensive microfilm of Nebraska newspapers. Chronicling America has digitized selections.

source_profiles.source_name: Union Pacific Railroad Land Records -- Platte County
source_profiles.repository: Union Pacific Historical Collection / History Nebraska / Nebraska State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Union Pacific Railroad land grant and sale records for Platte County. The UP received alternate sections of land along its right-of-way. Land department records document sales to settlers, town lot sales, and colony arrangements. UP Historical Collection at History Nebraska.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Polk County formed 1856 from unorganized territory. County seat is Osceola. Central Nebraska. Stromsburg area is a primary Swedish settlement. Big Blue River valley.
county_metadata.research_tips: Polk County (formed 1856, seat Osceola) records are at the courthouse in Osceola. Swedish settlers were particularly numerous in Polk County -- the Stromsburg area was one of Nebraska's primary Swedish settlements. Swedish Lutheran church records (Augustana Synod) are essential for baptisms, marriages, and burials. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Stromsburg's Swedish Heritage Festival reflects the enduring Swedish identity. Czech and German settlers were also present.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Polk County. County Register of Deeds in Osceola holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Red Willow County formed 1873 from unorganized territory. County seat is McCook. Southwestern Nebraska along the Republican River. Burlington Railroad division point. Senator George Norris home.
county_metadata.research_tips: Red Willow County (formed 1873, seat McCook) in southwestern Nebraska was carved from unorganized territory. Records are at the courthouse in McCook. McCook was a major Burlington Railroad division point. Senator George Norris, a progressive leader, was from McCook. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. The Republican River valley influenced settlement. German, Anglo-American, and Scandinavian settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The 1885 state census is available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. McCook newspapers from the 1880s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Red Willow County. County Register of Deeds in McCook holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Richardson County formed 1854 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Falls City. Extreme southeastern corner. Iowa, Sac and Fox Mission (1837). Sac and Fox reservation area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Richardson County (formed 1854, seat Falls City) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties in the extreme southeastern corner. Records are at the courthouse in Falls City. Being an original county, Richardson has some of the earliest records in the state. The Iowa, Sac and Fox Mission (1837) predates the territory. The Richardson County Historical Society preserves local materials. Richardson County borders Kansas and Missouri. Homestead and pre-emption entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. German, Irish, and Anglo-American settlers predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds territorial-era records. Falls City newspapers from the 1850s are among the earliest in Nebraska. The Sac and Fox reservation area in the county has Native American records at NARA Kansas City.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Richardson County. County Register of Deeds in Falls City holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rock County formed 1888 from Brown County. County seat is Bassett. North-central Nebraska. Niobrara River area. Ranching and farming.
county_metadata.research_tips: Rock County (formed 1888, seat Bassett) in north-central Nebraska was carved from Brown County. Records are at the courthouse in Bassett. Settlement came through homesteading and ranching in the 1880s. BLM GLO records document land entries. The Niobrara River valley influences the northern part of the county. Anglo-American and German settlers predominated. FamilySearch has limited coverage. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Researchers should check Brown and Holt county records for pre-1888 families.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Rock County. County Register of Deeds in Bassett holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Kinkaid Act Homestead Records -- Rock County
source_profiles.repository: NARA / BLM GLO Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Kinkaid Act (1904) homestead records for Rock County. The Kinkaid Act allowed 640-acre homestead claims in the Sandhills and other western Nebraska counties, recognizing that 160 acres was insufficient for ranching in semi-arid grasslands. Original case files at NARA; patents at BLM GLO.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Saline County formed 1855 as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Wilber. Official 'Czech Capital of the USA.' Strongest Czech/Bohemian heritage in Nebraska. Crete (Doane University).
county_metadata.research_tips: Saline County (formed 1855, seat Wilber) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties. Records are at the courthouse in Wilber. Saline County has the strongest Czech/Bohemian heritage in Nebraska -- Wilber is the official 'Czech Capital of the USA.' Czech Catholic and Czech Brethren church records are essential. The ZCBJ (Western Bohemian Fraternal Association) had major lodges here. The Saline County Historical Society and Wilber Czech Museum preserve heritage materials. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds Czech heritage collections. German settlers also contributed to the population. Crete (home of Doane University) was another significant community.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Saline County. County Register of Deeds in Wilber holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sarpy County formed 1857 from Douglas County. County seat is Papillion. Part of Omaha metropolitan area. Bellevue (1822), Nebraska's oldest continuous settlement. Offutt Air Force Base (formerly Fort Crook).
county_metadata.research_tips: Sarpy County (formed 1857, seat Papillion) records are at the courthouse in Papillion. Sarpy County is part of the Omaha metropolitan area and is Nebraska's third most populous county. Named for Peter Sarpy, a fur trader. Bellevue, the oldest continuous settlement in Nebraska (1822), is in Sarpy County. Offutt Air Force Base (formerly Fort Crook, 1896) has significant military history -- records at NARA. The Sarpy County Museum has local materials. Homestead entries at BLM GLO and early pre-emption claims document settlers. German, Irish, and Anglo-American families predominated. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Bellevue's early fur trade and missionary records predate the territorial period.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Sarpy County. County Register of Deeds in Papillion holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: History Nebraska (NSHS) holds microfilm copies of Sarpy County records including deed indexes, probate records, marriage records, county board proceedings, and selected court records. Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Sarpy County history.

source_profiles.source_name: Fort Crook / Offutt AFB Records -- Sarpy County
source_profiles.repository: NARA / History Nebraska
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Military records for Fort Crook (1896-present, renamed Offutt AFB). Post returns, muster rolls, hospital records, and personnel files at NARA. History Nebraska may hold supplementary materials. Military post records document soldiers, dependents, civilian employees, and local interactions.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Saunders County formed 1856 from Douglas and Lancaster counties. County seat is Wahoo. Strong Czech/Bohemian and Swedish heritage. Prague and Malmo settlements.
county_metadata.research_tips: Saunders County (formed 1856, seat Wahoo) records are at the courthouse in Wahoo. Saunders County has strong Czech/Bohemian and Swedish heritage. The Wahoo and Prague areas attracted Czech immigrants; Malmo and Mead had Swedish settlers. Czech and Swedish church records are important sources. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document original settlers. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. The Saunders County Historical Society in Wahoo preserves local materials. Wahoo newspapers from the 1870s are valuable.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Saunders County. County Register of Deeds in Wahoo holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Deed Records -- Register of Deeds
source_profiles.repository: Saunders County Register of Deeds, Wahoo
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1856 to present. Nebraska is a public land state; initial federal patents are at BLM GLO (glorecords.blm.gov). Subsequent transfers recorded at the county Register of Deeds. Includes deeds, mortgages, liens, and plats.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Records intact from county formation

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Marriage Records -- County Court
source_profiles.repository: Saunders County Court, Wahoo
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage records from 1856. Nebraska marriage licenses issued by county judges (County Court). State registration began 1909. Earlier marriages recorded at county level. FamilySearch has indexed many Nebraska marriages.

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Probate and Estate Records -- County Court
source_profiles.repository: Saunders County Court, Wahoo
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1856. Nebraska probate is handled by the County Court. Records include wills, letters of administration, estate inventories, guardianship files, and adoption records.

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Federal and State Census Records
source_profiles.repository: NARA / FamilySearch / Ancestry
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census records for Saunders County (1860-1950). The 1890 census was destroyed by fire. Nebraska conducted a state census in 1885 (available on microfilm). Territorial censuses (1854-1856, 1860) exist for early counties. Available through FamilySearch and Ancestry.
source_profiles.survival_notes: 1890 federal census destroyed nationally; Nebraska 1885 state census survives

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Federal Land Patents -- BLM GLO
source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Saunders County including homestead entries, pre-emption claims, timber culture entries, cash sales, and military warrant locations. Nebraska is a public land state -- all original land transfers from federal ownership are documented here. Searchable online by name and location.

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Cemetery Records -- Find A Grave / Historical Society
source_profiles.repository: Find A Grave / Saunders County Historical Society / Nebraska State Genealogical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Cemetery transcriptions and burial records for Saunders County. Find A Grave has growing coverage. Township, church, and family cemeteries are common in rural Nebraska. County historical societies and the Nebraska State Genealogical Society have published cemetery transcription volumes.

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Records on Microfilm -- History Nebraska
source_profiles.repository: History Nebraska, Lincoln
source_profiles.coverage_notes: History Nebraska (NSHS) holds microfilm copies of Saunders County records including deed indexes, probate records, marriage records, county board proceedings, and selected court records. Also holds manuscript collections, photographs, and maps related to Saunders County history.

source_profiles.source_name: Saunders County Czech/Bohemian Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Omaha / Diocese of Lincoln / FamilySearch / ZCBJ lodges
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Czech Catholic and Czech Brethren (ZCBJ) church records for Saunders County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Many Czech parishes maintained records in Czech language. FamilySearch has microfilmed some collections.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Scotts Bluff County formed 1888 from Cheyenne County. County seat is Gering. Major Panhandle county. Scotts Bluff National Monument (Oregon Trail). North Platte Valley irrigation. Sugar beet farming.
county_metadata.research_tips: Scotts Bluff County (formed 1888, seat Gering) is a major Panhandle county. Records are at the courthouse in Gering. Scottsbluff (the city) is the largest community and commercial center of the western Panhandle. Scotts Bluff National Monument preserves the Oregon Trail landmark. The Legacy of the Plains Museum in Gering has regional collections. Sugar beet farming in the North Platte Valley attracted German-Russian, Hispanic/Latino, and Japanese workers. Homestead and irrigation project records at BLM GLO document settlers. The North Platte Project (1904 Reclamation Act) transformed the valley. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Scottsbluff/Gering newspapers from the 1890s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Scotts Bluff County. County Register of Deeds in Gering holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.

source_profiles.source_name: Scotts Bluff County German Lutheran and Catholic Church Records
source_profiles.repository: Archdiocese of Omaha / Diocese of Lincoln / Diocese of Grand Island / FamilySearch
source_profiles.coverage_notes: German Lutheran and Catholic church records for Scotts Bluff County congregations. Baptisms, confirmations, marriages, and burials. Records often in German. German-Russian (Volga German) congregations may have separate records.

source_profiles.source_name: Scotts Bluff County Historic Newspapers
source_profiles.repository: History Nebraska / Library of Congress / Newspapers.com
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Historic newspapers for Scotts Bluff County communities. Obituaries, legal notices, marriage announcements, homestead proof notices, estate settlements, and community news. History Nebraska holds extensive microfilm of Nebraska newspapers. Chronicling America has digitized selections.

source_profiles.source_name: Union Pacific Railroad Land Records -- Scotts Bluff County
source_profiles.repository: Union Pacific Historical Collection / History Nebraska / Nebraska State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Union Pacific Railroad land grant and sale records for Scotts Bluff County. The UP received alternate sections of land along its right-of-way. Land department records document sales to settlers, town lot sales, and colony arrangements. UP Historical Collection at History Nebraska.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Seward County formed 1855 (as Greene, renamed) as original Nebraska Territory county. County seat is Seward. Concordia University (Lutheran). Strong German Lutheran heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: Seward County (formed 1855 as Greene, renamed Seward, seat Seward) is one of the original Nebraska Territory counties. Records are at the courthouse in Seward. Concordia University (Lutheran, 1894) is in Seward. Homestead entries at BLM GLO document early settlers. German Lutheran settlers were particularly numerous. Lutheran church records are important sources. FamilySearch has microfilmed county records. The territorial and 1885 state censuses are available. History Nebraska holds supplementary materials. Seward's Fourth of July celebration (oldest in Nebraska) reflects its civic history. Seward newspapers from the 1860s-1870s are valuable sources.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: BLM GLO patents for original federal land entries in Seward County. County Register of Deeds in Seward holds subsequent land transfers. Many counties do not have online access to county-level land records.
